steps.title: One attention head
steps.body: A transformer attention head reads each residual-stream vector three ways: as a query, a key, and a value. The query asks what to look for, keys decide which positions match, and values are the vectors that get mixed back in.

steps.title: Roles
steps.prompt: In scaled dot-product attention, what gets softmaxed?
choices: The dot products between one query and all keys.
The value vectors after they are summed.
explanations: Correct. Those scores become attention weights over positions.
No. Residual vectors are first transformed into queries, keys, and values.
Not quite. Values are mixed using the weights produced by the softmax.

steps.title: Value mixing
steps.prompt: If one attention weight becomes much larger than the others, what happens to the output for that query position?
choices: The output is pulled toward that position's value vector.
explanations: Correct. The output is a weighted sum of value vectors.
No. Keys are still used to compute the scores.
No. Changing Q changes the query and therefore the scores.
